chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ وَفَاتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 111 – Nazool (Arrival) of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> and his<sup>saww</sup> demise

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (253/ 5)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (253/ 5)
text: قِيلَ لَهُ إِنَّهُمْ يَزْعُمُونَ أَنَّ أَبَا طَالِبٍ كَانَ كَافِراً فَقَالَ كَذَبُوا كَيْفَ يَكُونُ كَافِراً وَ هُوَ يَقُولُ أَ لَمْ تَعْلَمُوا أَنَّا وَجَدْنَا مُحَمَّداً نَبِيّاً كَمُوسَى خُطَّ فِي أَوَّلِ الْكُتُبِ .

وَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ كَيْفَ يَكُونُ أَبُو طَالِبٍ كَافِراً وَ هُوَ يَقُولُ . لَقَدْ عَلِمُوا أَنَّ ابْنَنَا لَا مُكَذَّبٌ لَدَيْنَا وَ لَا يَعْبَأُ بِقِيلِ الْأَبَاطِلِ‏وَ أَبْيَضُ يُسْتَسْقَى الْغَمَامُ بِوَجْهِهِ ثِمَالُ الْيَتَامَى عِصْمَةٌ لِلْأَرَامِلِ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad and Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Is’haq, from Bakr Bin Muhammad Al Azady,

(It has been narrated) from Is’haq son of Ja’far<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>, from his father<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>, said, ‘It was said to him<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>, ‘They (people) are claiming that Abu Talib<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup><sup style="font-weight: normal">ws</sup> was a disbeliever’. So he<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup><sup style="font-weight: normal">ws</sup> said: ‘They are lying! How can he<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup><sup style="font-weight: normal">ws</sup> happen to be a disbeliever and he<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup><sup style="font-weight: normal">ws</sup> was saying: ‘Are you not knowing that we<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup><sup style="font-weight: normal">ws</sup> found Muhammad<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> as a Prophet<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup><sup style="font-weight: normal">ws</sup> like Musa<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup>, written in the former Books?’.

And in another Hadeeth – ‘How can Abu Talib<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> happen to be a disbeliever and he<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> was saying (in prose): ‘They (people) have know that our<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> son<sup>saww</sup> neither lies from us<sup>asws</sup>, nor can he<sup>saww</sup> be faulted with the words of falsehood, and he<sup>saww</sup> is white, the clouds quench by his<sup>saww</sup> face, a protector of the orphans and chastity of the widows’.
en.sarwar: 28. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hisham ibn Salim from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “The case of abu Talib islike the case of the people of the cave who hid their faith and expressed polytheism. Allah gave them twice as much reward.”
